2026-04-21T15:23:29.4906565Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T15:23:29.4907149Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T15:23:29.4907591Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T15:23:29.4907953Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T15:23:29.4908260Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T15:23:29.4908493Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T15:23:29.4908648Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-21T15:23:29.4909221Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T15:23:29.4909956Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The fix ensures these methods now consistently surface `RequestFailedException` for all error conditions, conforming to the SDK error-handling contract.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `GetEntityAsync` and `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` are entirely absent from the README\\u0026#39;s **Examples** section — there is no \\u0026#34;Get a single entity\\u0026#34; example showing how to fetch an entity by partition key and row key\\n- The **Troubleshooting** section only covers `RequestFailedException` for a duplicate-table create conflict; it does not demonstrate catching `RequestFailedException` when retrieving an entity (e.g., 404 Not Found)\\n- No test-backed snippet exists for `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, making it harder for the snippet tooling to inject a live example into the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo existing files need to be removed or restructured; the changes are additions only.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Snippet A — Get a single entity (async)\\n\\n**Test file to create or extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new test method (or add to an existing `Sample4_*` file) that demonstrates `GetEntityAsync`. Wrap the sample code in a named region:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task GetEntityAsync()\\n{\\n    // ... setup: create table and add an entity ...\\n\\n    #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync\\n    // Fetch a single entity by its partition key and row key.\\n    TableEntity entity = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync(\\n        partitionKey=[REDACTED]        rowKey=[REDACTED]    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Product: {entity.GetString(\\u0026#34;Product\\u0026#34;)}, Price: {entity.GetDouble(\\u0026#34;Price\\u0026#34;)}\\u0026#34;);\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Add the new snippet under the `## Examples` → `### Query table entities` section (or add a new `### Get a table entity` subsection before that section), referencing:\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync\\n```\\n````\\n\\n#### Snippet B — Get entity if exists (async)\\n\\n**Test file to create or extend:** same `Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs` file\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task GetEntityIfExistsAsync()\\n{\\n    // ... setup ...\\n\\n    #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityIfExistsAsync\\n    // Try to fetch a single entity; returns null if not found rather than throwing.\\n    NullableResponse response = await tableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync(\\n        partitionKey=[REDACTED]        rowKey=[REDACTED]    if (response.HasValue)\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Found: {response.Value.RowKey}\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n    else\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;Entity not found.\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Immediately after the `GetEntityAsync` snippet.\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityIfExistsAsync\\n```\\n````\\n\\n#### Snippet C — RequestFailedException when getting an entity (troubleshooting)\\n\\n**Test file to extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample7_Troubleshooting.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new test method demonstrating error handling for `GetEntityAsync`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task GetEntityNotFoundAsync()\\n{\\n    // ... setup ...\\n\\n    #region Snippet:GetEntityNotFoundAsync\\n    try\\n    {\\n        TableEntity entity = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync(\\n            partitionKey=[REDACTED]            rowKey=[REDACTED]    }\\n    catch (RequestFailedException ex) when (ex.Status == (int)HttpStatusCode.NotFound)\\n    {\\n        Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Entity not found: {ex.Message}\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Add under `## Troubleshooting`, after the existing `CreateDuplicateTable` snippet, with a short introductory sentence:\\n\\n\\u0026gt; To check whether an entity exists without an exception, use `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`. When using `GetEntityAsync`, a `RequestFailedException` with status `404` is thrown if the entity does not exist.\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:GetEntityNotFoundAsync\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all six required top-level sections. The sections that need content added are:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---------|--------|\\n| Examples | Missing `GetEntityAsync` / `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` examples |\\n| Troubleshooting | Only covers table creation conflict; missing entity retrieval error handling |\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tables` — confirm README receives the new snippet content\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tables` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; The fix ensures these methods now consistently surface `RequestFailedException` for all error conditions, conforming to the SDK error-handling contract.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `GetEntityAsync` and `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` are entirely absent from the README\\u0026#39;s **Examples** section — there is no \\u0026#34;Get a single entity\\u0026#34; example showing how to fetch an entity by partition key and row key\\n- The **Troubleshooting** section only covers `RequestFailedException` for a duplicate-table create conflict; it does not demonstrate catching `RequestFailedException` when retrieving an entity (e.g., 404 Not Found)\\n- No test-backed snippet exists for `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, making it harder for the snippet tooling to inject a live example into the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\nNo existing files need to be removed or restructured; the changes are additions only.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Snippet A — Get a single entity (async)\\n\\n**Test file to create or extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new test method (or add to an existing `Sample4_*` file) that demonstrates `GetEntityAsync`.
